sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) a species of gem; according to many sardonyx or onyx, so called from its resemblance to a human nail, Gen 2:12 Exo 28:9, 20 Exo 28:20 35:9 , 27 Exo 35:27 Job 28:16 Eze 28:13. See Braun, De Vestitu Sacerd. Hebr. ii. 18. J. D. Michaëlis (Supplemm. p. 2289) supposes it to be the onyx with whitish lines, comparing the Arab. مُسَهَّمُ a striped garment.

(2) [Shoham], pr.n. m. 1Ch 24:27.
